summary: The draft has the right raw material — a §4A subsystem DAG, import-direction rules, a parallel-tracks list, and a "Shared Contracts To Freeze First" set — but the DAG's notation is ambiguous and visually reads as a single linear chain, which hides the real cross-edges (most importantly model-gateway fanning out to runtime AND verifier AND selection) and never states which subsystems share NO dependency path. The OUTLINE has a richer "Can Start After / Integrates At" table than the DRAFT §4A, but neither maps the DAG onto the 4 ownership surfaces named in STAKEHOLDERS.md (kernel/runtime, selection/ML, verifier council, demo/observability), so tasks-gen cannot derive build TRACKS from §4A alone. There is no "Appendix A" of models; the freeze-set lives in §6 `contracts` / "Shared Contracts To Freeze First". The import-direction rules ARE explicit and locked. The chief gaps are: (1) the DAG edge list is incomplete/ambiguous and must be made an explicit edge set; (2) the true independent-tracks callout (which pairs can fork in parallel) is missing; (3) cross-edges that the indented list hides need to be surfaced; (4) the DAG must be tied to the 4 ownership surfaces and to the exact contract objects whose freeze unblocks each fork.

findings.finding: The §4A DAG is written as an indented list under a single 'shared-contracts ->' header, which renders as a LINEAR CHAIN (persistence -> model-gateway -> runtime -> verifier -> selection -> reproduction -> projection -> api -> dashboard). This notation is ambiguous (is each line shared-contracts->X, or X->next?) and hides the real fan-out edges. tasks-gen cannot reliably derive parallel tracks from an ambiguous chain; it will serialize work that is actually parallelizable.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4A lines 107-129; identical text in ARCHITECTURE_OUTLINE.md 'Subsystem Dependency DAG' lines 45-74
findings.proposed_fix: Replace the indented list with an explicit edge set, one 'A -> B' per line with NO nesting, e.g.: shared-contracts -> {persistence, model-gateway, runtime-kernel, verifier-council, selection-scoring, reproduction-fusion, projection-builders, observability-adapter, lineage-projection}; persistence -> {runtime-kernel, projection-builders, neo4j-spike}; model-gateway -> {runtime-kernel, verifier-council, selection-scoring}; runtime-kernel -> {verifier-council, selection-scoring, reproduction-fusion, event-store(write)}; verifier-council -> selection-scoring; selection-scoring -> reproduction-fusion; reproduction-fusion -> runtime-kernel(next generation); projection-builders -> {backend-api, frontend-dashboard}; backend-api -> frontend-dashboard; observability-adapter -> model-gateway. State once that nesting implies nothing; only explicit edges are dependencies.

findings.finding: The hidden cross-edge the linear chain conceals: model-gateway is a dependency of runtime-kernel AND verifier-council AND selection-scoring (three of the four ownership surfaces). The OUTLINE Parallel Tracks table confirms this (model gateway 'Integrates At: Runtime + verifier'; verifier 'Can Start After: ... + model gateway'; selection 'Candidate/check/score schemas') yet the DAG draws model-gateway as one node in a single line. This makes model-gateway a true shared-fork blocker, not a mid-chain node, and it must be frozen/stubbed before three teams can proceed.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4A lines 119-121 (only shows shared-contracts -> observability -> model-gateway and the chain) vs OUTLINE 'Parallel Build Tracks' lines 94,96 ('Model gateway ... Integrates At: Runtime + verifier'; 'Verifier/checks Can Start After: ... + model gateway')
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit edges model-gateway -> verifier-council and model-gateway -> selection-scoring (in addition to model-gateway -> runtime-kernel which line 121/68-69 already implies). Then add a callout: 'model-gateway is a shared dependency of 3 of the 4 ownership surfaces; its request/response + ProviderCapability contract is on the freeze-first list, and a recorded/fake gateway adapter must exist before runtime, verifier, and selection fork.'

findings.finding: Neither §4A nor the OUTLINE states which subsystems share NO dependency path after the contracts freeze — i.e. the true independent parallel tracks. The audit task explicitly asks for this callout and it is absent. Without it, tasks-gen cannot assert that two tracks can run concurrently; it must conservatively serialize.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4A 'Parallel Tracks' lines 140-150 list subsystems but assert no independence relationships; OUTLINE 'Parallel Build Tracks' lines 88-100 give 'Can Start After' but never 'has no path to'.
findings.proposed_fix: Add an explicit 'Independent Tracks (no shared dependency path after freeze)' callout, e.g.: After shared-contracts + model-gateway stub + event-store schema are frozen, these run with no edge between them: (A) runtime-kernel + reproduction-fusion; (B) verifier-council + subtype checks; (C) selection-scoring/novelty; (D) projection-builders + frontend-dashboard (against fixture projections); (E) observability-adapter; (F) neo4j-spike (against sample lineage export). Note the only edges crossing these groups converge at the runtime owner's integration point (generation loop) and the demo owner's integration point (live projection feed). Verifier and selection DO share model-gateway and the candidate/check/score contracts, so they are independent only after those are frozen.

findings.finding: The DAG is not mapped onto the 4 ownership surfaces named in STAKEHOLDERS.md (kernel/runtime, selection/ML, verifier council, demo/observability). The OUTLINE has 'Integration Merge Points' that assign integration ownership, but the DAG nodes (9-10 of them) are never grouped under the 4 owners, so a reader cannot see which owner blocks which. The audit's premise is that §2.5/§4A must let tasks-gen derive build tracks per surface.
findings.evidence: STAKEHOLDERS.md lines 12-15 name the 4 owners; OUTLINE 'Integration Merge Points' lines 118-126 assign 4 owners but the DAG (OUTLINE 45-74 / DRAFT 107-129) lists ungrouped nodes; no surface->node mapping table exists.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a surface-ownership mapping to §4A: kernel/runtime owner = {shared-contracts authoring lead, runtime-kernel, reproduction-fusion, event-store, model-gateway integration}; selection/ML owner = {selection-scoring, novelty, fitness policy}; verifier council owner = {verifier-council, subtype checks, evidence schemas}; demo/observability owner = {projection-builders, backend-api, frontend-dashboard, observability-adapter, neo4j-spike}. Then each owner's track is the subgraph reachable within their nodes plus the frozen contracts they consume.

findings.finding: There is no 'Appendix A' of models anywhere in the planning set, yet the freeze-first contract objects are the load-bearing fork gate. The freeze set is split across two places (OUTLINE 'Shared Contracts To Freeze First' and DRAFT §6 `contracts`) and is NOT cross-referenced to which DAG edges each object sits on, so it is unclear which contract unblocks which fork.
findings.evidence: No 'Appendix' match in docs/planning/*.md (grep returned 0); freeze set in OUTLINE lines 102-116 and DRAFT §6 lines 178-192; canonical model shapes in DATA_MODEL.md lines 16-31, 79-115, 126-145.
findings.proposed_fix: In §4A, add a 'Contracts crossed by a DAG edge (freeze before forking)' table mapping each shared object to the edge(s) it gates: RunEventEnvelope -> every edge into/out of event-store and projection-builders; Agenome -> shared-contracts->runtime-kernel; CandidateIdea + subtype payloads -> runtime->verifier, runtime->selection, ->projection; CriticReview/CheckResult -> verifier->selection; FitnessScore + ScoringPolicy -> selection->reproduction; ModelGatewayRequest/Response + ProviderCapability -> model-gateway->{runtime,verifier,selection}; LineageGraphProjection -> projection-builders->{frontend,neo4j-spike}. This is the de-facto 'Appendix A'.

findings.finding: The DAG omits the reproduction-fusion -> runtime-kernel back-edge (next-generation feedback) that the OUTLINE Parallel Tracks table names ('Selection/scoring/reproduction ... Integrates At: Runtime next generation'). Drawn naively this is a cycle; if §4A is meant to be acyclic for track derivation, the feedback must be modeled as a runtime-internal call against the frozen FitnessScore/Agenome contracts, not a build-time dependency edge. Left implicit, tasks-gen may either create a phantom cycle or miss the integration handoff.
findings.evidence: OUTLINE 'Parallel Build Tracks' line 97 ('Selection/scoring/reproduction ... Integrates At: Runtime next generation') and DRAFT §5 invariant 'Replay uses persisted events' line 174; the §4A DAG (107-129) shows no such edge.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a note in §4A: 'reproduction-fusion feeds the next generation back into runtime-kernel at RUNTIME via the frozen Agenome/FitnessScore contracts; this is an integration handoff at the runtime owner's merge point, not a build-time import edge — the DAG remains acyclic for track derivation.'

findings.finding: The DRAFT §4A is strictly weaker than the OUTLINE on the same topic: the OUTLINE has a 'Can Start After / Owns / Integrates At' table (lines 88-100) and 'Integration Merge Points' (118-126), but DRAFT §4A 'Parallel Tracks' (140-150) is just a flat bullet list with no start/integrate metadata. The binding doc will inherit from the DRAFT, so the richer OUTLINE content risks being lost.
findings.proposed_fix: Port the OUTLINE 'Can Start After / Owns / Integrates At' table and 'Integration Merge Points' into DRAFT §4A verbatim so the finalized doc carries the start-gate and merge-point metadata, not just a bullet list.

findings.finding: The import-direction rules ARE explicit and locked (5 rules), satisfying the 'is the import-direction rule explicit?' probe — but they are stated as prose and not tied to the DAG node names, so they cannot be mechanically checked against the edge set.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4A 'Import Rules' lines 131-138; OUTLINE 'Import Direction Rules' lines 76-86 (locked).
findings.proposed_fix: Restate each import rule referencing DAG node names and add the contrapositive forbidden edges, e.g. 'runtime-kernel MUST NOT import provider-adapters, frontend-dashboard, or projection-builders; only shared-contracts + ModelGateway port.' This lets an eslint/dependency-cruiser boundary lint enforce §4A directly.

findings.finding: The package structure that the tracks fork along is still open ('monorepo with apps/web, apps/api, packages/contracts, or smaller single-app'). The chosen structure determines whether tracks are physically isolated (separate packages = clean parallel ownership) or share one app (merge-conflict-prone). This directly affects whether the §4A tracks can actually run in parallel.
findings.proposed_fix: Decision needed: package layout. Recommended default for 3-4 engineers + 4 ownership surfaces: pnpm monorepo with packages/contracts (frozen first, owned by runtime lead), apps/api (runtime + event-store + gateway + verifier + selection), apps/web (dashboard + projections client), and a thin packages/observability — gives each ownership surface its own buildable boundary while keeping contracts a single frozen package every track imports.

findings.finding: Job execution model (in-process worker vs queue library) is open, and it changes the DAG: a queue introduces a backend-api/worker seam between runtime-kernel and the generation/critic/check jobs that §4A does not currently draw.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_OUTLINE.md 'Open Section-Planning Questions' line 134; OUTLINE §10 row 'Run worker, generation jobs, critic/check jobs' line 28.
findings.proposed_fix: Decision needed: worker model. Recommended default for a 2-week MVP demo: single in-process async worker inside apps/api (no external queue), with idempotency keyed on event sequence; defer a real queue. This keeps the DAG free of an extra queue node and avoids cross-process replay complexity. Flag the queue as a named deferral.
